title: Sorting Array of Integers with Incorrect Sort Order

statement.markdown: Sort the given array of integers in ascending order. If an element is smaller than the next one, swap them. The function should return `true` if the array is sorted and `false` otherwise. This function uses the bubble sort algorithm.
statement.inputFormat: The input contains a single integer `n` followed by two space-separated integers.
statement.outputFormat: The output should contain `true` or `false` indicating whether the array is sorted or not.

examples.explanation: The array [1, 3, 5] is sorted in ascending order.
The array [1, 3, 5] is not sorted in ascending order.

hints.text: Bubble sort works by repeatedly swapping adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order.

editorial.title: Bubble Sort
editorial.intuition: We will use the bubble sort algorithm to sort the array.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach involves using the bubble sort algorithm and checking if the array is sorted after each swap.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal approach involves using the bubble sort algorithm and checking if the array is sorted after each pass.
editorial.dryRun: Bubble sort works by repeatedly swapping adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to update the swapped flag

starterCode.java: public class Solution {public boolean solve(int[] nums) { // TODO: implement}
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> bool: # TODO: implement

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: bool solve(vector<int>& nums) { for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); i++) { for (int j = 0; j < nums.size() - 1; j++) { if (nums[j] == nums[j + 1]) { return true; } } } return false; }
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution {public boolean solve(int[] nums) { for (int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < nums.length - 1; j++) { if (nums[j] == nums[j + 1]) { return true; } } } return false; }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> bool: for i in range(len(nums)): for j in range(len(nums) - 1): if nums[j] == nums[j + 1]: return True return False
buggyStarterCode.javascript: var solve = function(nums) { for (var i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { for (var j = 0; j < nums.length - 1; j++) { if (nums[j] == nums[j + 1]) { return true; } } } return false; }
